seoTitle: How to Use Jira Reports for Team Tracking
metaDescription: Learn how to open the Jira Reports hub, generate sprint burndown and velocity charts, and share dashboard insights with your team.
intro: Trying to gauge sprint health by eyeballing a board only goes so far. This guide covers navigating Jira's Reports hub, generating burndown and velocity charts, comparing planned versus actual work, and sharing the results with your team.

steps.heading: Open the Reports hub
steps.body: Navigate to the left sidebar menu in your Jira space and select the Reports option to bring up the centralized reporting view.

steps.heading: Generate sprint burndown or velocity charts
steps.body: Choose the agile reports section and select Sprint Burndown or Velocity Chart to see how your team is progressing against the plan.

steps.heading: Filter by sprint and adjust the timeline
steps.body: Select your current sprint and adjust the time axis so the chart reflects the exact period you want to review.

steps.heading: Compare planned versus actual work
steps.body: Toggle the capacity indicator overlay to spot where actual progress is deviating from what was originally planned.

steps.heading: Pin and share your metrics
steps.body: Pin your favorite charts to your team's dashboard, or export the data so stakeholders can see progress without digging into the reports hub themselves.

faq.question: Where can I find Jira reports?
faq.answer: Open your Jira space and select the Reports option from the left sidebar to access sprint, velocity, and other tracking charts.

faq.question: What's the difference between a burndown chart and a velocity chart?
faq.answer: A burndown chart tracks remaining work within a single sprint, while a velocity chart compares completed work across multiple sprints over time.

faq.question: Can I see planned versus actual work in Jira reports?
faq.answer: Yes, most agile reports let you toggle an overlay that compares planned capacity against actual logged progress.

faq.question: Can I share a Jira report with people who don't use Jira daily?
faq.answer: You can pin reports to a shared dashboard or export the data so stakeholders can review progress without navigating the reports hub.
